title: FIFA 2026 Ticket Prices: I Tracked Every Category — The $2,200 to $250,000 Spectrum
excerpt: I built a spreadsheet tracking every FIFA 2026 ticket price from $40 group stage seats to $250,000 Presidential Suites. Here's the complete breakdown of what you actually get at each level.
content:
I spent a weekend building a price spreadsheet for FIFA 2026 tickets.

Not because I'm obsessive. Because the official pricing is scattered across 4 different pages, 3 PDFs, and a Twitter thread from an account that may or may not be official.

Here's the complete picture. Every category, every price range, every match type.

## Category 4: The Budget Option (General Public)

**Price range: $40–$200**
**Available for: Group stage only**

Yes, there are $40 tickets to the World Cup. They exist. They are in the very back of very large stadiums. You'll watch mostly on the screen. You'll stand in lines for everything.

But you're in the building. And for $40, that's honestly incredible.

**Who it's for:** Locals who want to experience a World Cup match without breaking the bank.

## Category 3: Standard Seating

**Price range: $120–$600**
**Available for: All matches except Final**

These are the "good seats" that regular people buy. Lower bowl. Decent sightlines. You'll still queue for the bathroom and overpay for beer.

Group stage: $120–$300
Quarter-Final: $200–$450
Semi-Final: $300–$550
Final: Not available in this category — starts at Category 2.

## Category 2: Premium Standard

**Price range: $400–$900**
**Available for: All matches including Final**

Better sections. Better views. The Final in Category 2 runs about $800–$900. Still no VIP amenities — just a better seat.

## Category 1: Best Standard Seats

**Price range: $600–$1,800**
**Available for: All matches**

These are the front rows of the upper bowl or the prime lower bowl sections. The Final in Category 1 is around $1,600–$1,800.

At this price, you're paying for the view. You still buy your own hot dog.

## Hospitality Silver

**Price: $2,200–$4,500**
**Available for: All matches**

This is where VIP starts. Private stadium entrance. Shared lounge with open bar. Better food (buffet, but a good buffet).

**Honest take:** Worth it just for the private entrance. The Silver lounge is crowded on big match days, but you're not standing in the main security line.

## Hospitality Gold

**Price: $5,500–$9,000**
**Available for: All matches including Final**

The sweet spot. Dedicated hospitality area. Premium bar (top shelf included). Served meal, not buffet. Concierge support.

**Honest take:** If you can afford one step up from Silver, this is where the experience changes from "slightly better" to "genuinely different."

## Hospitality Platinum

**Price: $10,000–$18,000**
**Available for: All knockout matches**

Private lounge. Dedicated server at your seat. Pre-match field access. Priority exit. This is "I remember this for the rest of my life" territory.

**Honest take:** Field access is worth the upgrade alone. Standing on the pitch before 70,000 seats are filled is a moment you don't forget.

## Suite Level

**Price: $20,000–$55,000 (per suite, 4–8 guests)**
**Available for: All knockout matches, some group stage**

Private room. Glass front. Dedicated server. Gourmet catering. Your own bathroom. Your own bar.

**Honest take:** Best value per person if you have a group. $55,000 for 8 people is $6,875 each — cheaper than Platinum with a better experience.

## Presidential Suite

**Price: $85,000–$250,000**
**Available for: Final, Semi-Finals, Quarter-Finals**

The top tier. Private entrance through a door most people don't know exists. Rolls-Royce pickup. Personal chef. The best seats in the stadium.

The $250,000 version for the Final includes a 3-night hotel stay at a 5-star property and a dedicated concierge for the entire tournament.

**Honest take:** This isn't a ticket. It's a status symbol. If you can afford it, you already know whether it's worth it.

## The Chart You Actually Need

If you have $5,000 to spend: Get Gold.
If you have $10,000: Get Platinum.
If you have $20,000 with friends: Get a Suite.
If you're a billionaire: Presidential Suite.

I went Platinum for the Semi-Final and Silver for a group stage match. That combination gave me one unforgettable experience and one solid experience for less than the cost of a single Suite.

[Compare all ticket tiers](/tickets) — real-time pricing and availability.
